summary: Verified Mining Formula Math Consistency bug fix. All 18 backend tests passed. Frontend Mining Formula Breakdown section displays correctly with all formula components. Math is now consistent: network_rate = network_size × prc_per_user.
original_bug: Mining Formula math was wrong - Base + Network Bonus calculations were inconsistent. The Active Network count shown and the PRC/user and Network Rate values didn't match mathematically (71 x 5.275422 ≠ 395.66). Root cause: cache read using `or` operator treated 0 as falsy.

fix_verified.cache_read_fix: Changed from `or` operator to explicit `is None` checks for cached network values
fix_verified.new_api_fields: Added active_network, total_network_members, direct_referrals, network_cap to /api/mining/status/{uid} and /api/mining/rate-breakdown/{uid}
fix_verified.formula_transparency_ui: Added Mining Formula Breakdown section to Mining page showing all formula components

critical_code_review_comments: mining.py calculate_mining_rate() correctly uses `is None` check instead of `or` operator for cached_network
mining.py get_mining_status() returns all new fields: active_network, total_network_members, direct_referrals, network_cap
mining.py get_rate_breakdown() returns active_network and total_network_members fields
growth_economy.py get_growth_network_stats() also uses explicit None checks for cached values
Mining.js formulaData state correctly displays all formula components in Mining Formula section

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Mining Formula Breakdown section displays correctly with all elements)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Mining Formula Math bug fix verified. Backend returns consistent math: network_rate = network_size × prc_per_user. New fields (active_network, total_network_members, direct_referrals, network_cap) present in both /api/mining/status/{uid} and /api/mining/rate-breakdown/{uid}. Frontend Mining Formula Breakdown section shows all components transparently. Elite users can collect, explorer users can mine but cannot collect.

POST /api/mining/collect/d99f75fc-a9f6-4.detail: Elite subscription required to collect PRC. Upgrade to collect!

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Python's `or` operator treats 0 as falsy. When cached_network was 0 (valid value for users with no active network), the code `cached_network or user.get('_cached_network_size')` would skip the 0 and try to get another value, potentially returning stale data.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Changed to explicit `is None` checks: `if cached_network is None: cached_network = user.get('_cached_network_size')`
rca_of_the_issue.verification: Math now consistent: network_rate = network_size × prc_per_user exactly
